reasoning_model_1: The user command explicitly requested the model to read the specific sentence: 'Get out of the way, don't block my path!'. However, Model 1 generated: 'Get out of the way, don't you dare stand in my path, seriously?'. This represents a significant content hallucination and deviation from the script, violating the 'Content Accuracy' criterion where the voice content must match the script. Although the added words ('don't you dare', 'seriously') convey a rude attitude, the model failed the fundamental instruction to read the provided text exactly. According to the scoring standard, a score of 0 is assigned when voice content is clearly inconsistent with the script.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 generated the audio content: 'Get out of the way, don't block my path.', which is a perfect match for the requested text. In terms of the 'Attitude' dimension, the delivery of the imperative phrase follows the user's command to be rude. By maintaining content fidelity while delivering the line, Model 2 satisfies the core requirements of the task. Assuming the paralinguistic features correspond to the command (as the content is accurate and the context is clear), this model performs significantly better than the model that hallucinated the text.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner. Model 1 failed the primary constraint of the task by rewriting the script ('don't you dare stand in my path, seriously?') instead of reading the requested sentence ('don't block my path!'). While Model 1's rewriting attempted to enhance the rude tone, the violation of Content Accuracy renders the generation incorrect based on the evaluation criteria. Model 2 adhered to the script perfectly. Therefore, Model 2 demonstrates superior quality by following the user's explicit command regarding the text content.
